prompt:
Security-engineering ticket. Compliance wants every destructive event from our GitHub Enterprise Cloud audit log in the SIEM, and the current exporter is a cron'd curl that re-downloads the same first page every night and once POSTed our bearer token to a 'mirror' host somebody slipped into a Link header during a red-team exercise. Rewrite it properly as a C# library in this xunit project (net10.0, namespace GheAudit, no packages beyond what the test project already references). AuditStreamTests.cs is the acceptance contract.

Research before code — literally before changing code, use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official GitHub Enterprise Cloud docs: at minimum the REST reference for the enterprise audit-log endpoint (every query parameter and what the after/before cursors actually are) and the enterprise guide on using the audit log API (how pagination really works there, the phrase search syntax, what unit the timestamps are in, and the endpoint's own rate-limit budget). Only first-party docs.github.com pages count as sources. Our pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected. If the live docs contradict anything pinned, say so in your summary and leave fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests exercise:
- AuditLogClient(baseUrl, enterprise, token, httpClient, pace) with StreamAsync(AuditQuery, CancellationToken) returning IAsyncEnumerable<AuditEvent>. AuditQuery holds Phrase (a list of search qualifiers), Include, Order, PerPage, After. Build the documented query string — qualifiers join the way the guide's examples show, everything else percent-encoded, phrase omitted when empty. The exporter always requests ascending order and a full page of 100 (defaults on AuditQuery), sends the vnd accept media type, the CURRENT dated API version header, bearer auth, and a real User-Agent.
- Parse the response as a stream into AuditEvent records (DocumentId, Action, Actor, Org, TimestampMs — mind the documented timestamp unit; tolerate unknown fields) and walk the cursor pagination exactly as documented: follow the server's next URL verbatim, request the next page only after the current one is consumed (cancellation between pages must stop cleanly with no extra request), and REFUSE any next URL that isn't on the configured origin — typed AuditSecurityException naming the host, without ever contacting it or forwarding the token.
- On a rate-limited response, honor the documented pause guidance via the injected pace delegate (never Thread.Sleep/Task.Delay with real time) using the server's Retry-After, retry that exact URL once, and raise a typed AuditRateLimitException carrying RetryAfter if it happens again back-to-back. Other failures raise AuditApiException with the status and GitHub's message — no token, no auth header, ever.
- AuditExporter(client, ICheckpointStore store) with RunAsync(query, ct): load any stored cursor and resume from it, collect the full page set, and return an ExportResult (Events, Complete, Checkpoint, Error). The checkpoint — the after cursor proving everything before it was delivered — is saved through the store exactly once, only after a COMPLETE page set with new events; a 5xx mid-scan yields Complete=false with the partial events, a null checkpoint, the typed error attached, and no save. An up-to-date run (nothing new) completes without saving.

Acceptance: dotnet test — it runs a loopback HttpListener fake; no real GitHub, no real tokens. AuditStreamTests.cs, GheAuditStream.csproj, and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: Enterprise audit log REST API (GET /enterprises/{enterprise}/audit-log, X-GitHub-Api-Version 2026-03-10)

official_sources.title: REST API endpoints for the audit log - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /enterprises/{enterprise}/audit-log; query parameters phrase, include (web default | git | all), after ('A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for events after this cursor.'), before, order (desc default | asc), per_page (default 30), page; requires read:audit_log scope; 200 response of event objects with @timestamp, action, actor, actor_id, org, org_id, created_at, _document_id, business fields

official_sources.title: Using the audit log API for your enterprise - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: cursor-based pagination through the Link header's after/before URLs rather than page numbers; 'Timestamps and date fields in the API response are measured in UTC epoch milliseconds'; phrase search syntax joining qualifiers like action: and created:>= with '+'; the 1,750 queries per hour per user+IP rate limit and the guidance to pause before retrying on 403/429

official_sources.title: API versions - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: X-GitHub-Api-Version header; supported versions 2026-03-10 (latest) and 2022-11-28 (default when the header is omitted); unsupported versions receive 410 Gone

research.language_rationale: The SIEM forwarder this exporter feeds is a .NET service, and the enterprise audit log is a documented raw-REST surface (GitHub's official Octokit .NET SDK does not cover the enterprise audit-log endpoint), so HttpClient with System.Text.Json async stream parsing and IAsyncEnumerable is the idiomatic, credible C# integration; xunit on net10.0 hosts the hermetic loopback listener.
